original_title: JAX-vectorize Boltzmann plot fitting across elements. COMPOSE: JAX-vectorize Boltzmann fitting in cflibs/inversion/boltzmann.py. GEMINI DEEP RESEARCH RECOMMENDATION: Discard RANSAC and sigma-clipping for the JAX path — their dynamic array shapes conflict with XLA compilation (no dynamic shapes allowed in jit). Instead, compose Optax huber_loss with JAX
required_outcomes: JAX-vectorize Boltzmann plot fitting across elements. COMPOSE: JAX-vectorize Boltzmann fitting in cflibs/inversion/boltzmann.py. GEMINI DEEP RESEARCH RECOMMENDATION: Discard RANSAC and sigma-clipping for the JAX path — their dynamic array shapes conflict with XLA compilation (no dynamic shapes allowed in jit). Instead, compose Optax huber_loss with JAX

original_title: GPU-accelerate Voigt profile computation via JAX vmap. BUILD (Derived): GPU-accelerate Voigt profile in cflibs/radiation/profiles.py. GEMINI DEEP RESEARCH RECOMMENDATION: Do NOT use Zaghloul 2024 (causes GPU warp divergence due to branching). Instead, extract the branchless Faddeeva/Weideman formulation from ExoJAX opacity module (exojax.opacity). Imple
required_outcomes: GPU-accelerate Voigt profile computation via JAX vmap. BUILD (Derived): GPU-accelerate Voigt profile in cflibs/radiation/profiles.py. GEMINI DEEP RESEARCH RECOMMENDATION: Do NOT use Zaghloul 2024 (causes GPU warp divergence due to branching). original_title: Diagnose accuracy bottleneck: element ID vs solver vs closure. The ablation shows RMSEP ~0.45 with minimal differentiation across solver configs. Root cause analysis needed: (1) Is element ID the bottleneck? Recall=0.68 means 32% of expected elements are missed. (2) Are wrong elements being included (precision=0.35)? (3) Is the solver converging to wrong T? Man
required_outcomes: Diagnose accuracy bottleneck: element ID vs solver vs closure. The ablation shows RMSEP ~0.45 with minimal differentiation across solver configs. original_title: Fix element identification false positives (precision=0.35). ALIAS identification produces too many false positives (precision 0.346). Atmospheric/contaminant elements (N, O, Na, K, Ca, Ar) are detected in nearly every spectrum. Solutions: (1) Implement atmospheric-element exclusion filter, (2) Raise ALIAS detection threshold, (3) Use multi-line consistency c
required_outcomes: Fix element identification false positives (precision=0.35). ALIAS identification produces too many false positives (precision 0.346). Atmospheric/contaminant elements (N, O, Na, K, Ca, Ar) are detected in nearly every spectrum. Solutions: (1) Implement atmospheric-element exclusion filter, (2) Raise ALIAS detection threshold, (3) Use multi-line consistency c

original_title: Fix solver convergence failures (T clamped at 49922K). Multiple minerals show solver converging to T=49922K (max clamp), indicating the Boltzmann slope is positive or near-zero. This happens when: (1) Too few lines per element for reliable fit, (2) Self-absorbed lines flatten the Boltzmann plot, (3) Wrong lines identified. Solutions: (1) Require minimum
required_outcomes: Fix solver convergence failures (T clamped at 49922K). Multiple minerals show solver converging to T=49922K (max clamp), indicating the Boltzmann slope is positive or near-zero. This happens when: (1) Too few lines per element for reliable fit, (2) Self-absorbed lines flatten the Boltzmann plot, (3) Wrong lines identified. Solutions: (1) Require minimum

original_title: Add accuracy quality gate to swarm profile. Add scripts/run_accuracy_ablation.py as a quality gate in .swarm/profile.toml. Every PR that touches cflibs/inversion/ must not regress RMSEP. Gate: run ablation with --max-spectra 10 --skip-voigt (fast, 8 configs × 10 spectra). Fail if best-config RMSEP increases by >0.01 from baseline. Store basel
required_outcomes: Add accuracy quality gate to swarm profile. Add scripts/run_accuracy_ablation.py as a quality gate in .swarm/profile.toml. Every PR that touches cflibs/inversion/ must not regress RMSEP. Gate: run ablation with --max-spectra 10 --skip-voigt (fast, 8 configs × 10 spectra). Fail if best-config RMSEP increases by >0.01 from baseline. Store basel

original_title: Improve self-absorption handling for resonance lines. Current CD-SB correction exists but ablation shows minimal impact (RMSEP 0.4501 vs 0.4541). Issues: (1) CD-SB may not be triggering for the right lines, (2) Resonance line exclusion may be more effective than correction, (3) Escape factor thresholds may need tuning. Solutions: (1) Auto-detect resona
required_outcomes: Improve self-absorption handling for resonance lines. Current CD-SB correction exists but ablation shows minimal impact (RMSEP 0.4501 vs 0.4541). original_title: Download SuperCam calibration targets from PDS archive. Download SuperCam calibrated LIBS data products from Mars 2020 PDS archive: pds-geosciences.wustl.edu/m2020/m2020-m-supercam-libs-5-rdr-v1/. Parse with cflibs/pds/supercam.py. Store in data/supercam_targets/. Three spectrometers: UV 245-340nm, VIO 385-465nm, VNIR 536-853nm.
required_outcomes: Download SuperCam calibration targets from PDS archive. Download SuperCam calibrated LIBS data products from Mars 2020 PDS archive: pds-geosciences.wustl.edu/m2020/m2020-m-supercam-libs-5-rdr-v1/. Parse with cflibs/pds/supercam.py. Store in data/supercam_targets/. Three spectrometers: UV 245-340nm, VIO 385-465nm, VNIR 536-853nm.
